programmes.show_description: Rob Beckett, Susan Calman, Hal Cruttenden and Romesh Ranganathan join the regulars to get their teeth into the week's news - including Luis Suarez's fang-tastic World Cup.
Rob Brydon hosts the sly panel show as David Mitchell, Lee Mack, Fiona Bruce, Micky Flanagan, Steve Jones and Claudia Winkleman tell implausible truths and believable lies.

programmes.show_name: Would I Lie To You At Christmas
programmes.show_description: Sorting the fact from the fiction in this festive special are guests Jim Broadbent, Ardal O'Hanlon, Rose Matafeo and Angela Rippon.
Josh Widdicombe and James Acaster host as more celebrities attempt to put the unreal into reality - their guests this time are Bill Bailey, Jo Brand, Harriet Kemsley and Darren Harriott.
Josh Widdicombe and James Acaster present another round of scenarios we may or may not like to see - their guests this time are Jack Dee, Guz Khan, Suzi Ruffell and Rose Johnson.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.

programmes.show_name: Dave Gorman: Modern Life Is Goodish
programmes.show_description: The Dirt Collects There: Impish original comedy from Dave Gorman, who muses on life's little foibles - including light bulbs, gravy boats, dot-to-dot books and Lego.
It's Not All Rainbows and Butterflies: Armed police and drug squad officers arrest three men after a dangerous social media post, and a man fails a breathalyser after crashing his moped.
The Law's The Law: Customs officers fish hash bales out of the sea and incinerate them, discarded smugglers' jerry cans wash up on a beach, and cops stop a car laden with extra fuel.
Documentary series. A crack team in Victoria races to build the state's VLocity trains of the future, and slippery tracks and kangaroos throw a coal train off its path.
Sicily and Puglia: A colourful culinary tour of the Mediterranean. Rick continues his travels in Sicily and enjoys the ambience of Catania's busy fish market - he then travels on to Puglia.
Thousands of dollars of cartel cash are found hidden in an engine, an intoxicated 17-year-old requires urgent medical treatment, and cocaine is found in bags of rubbish.
Cocaine is found hidden inside the wall of a refrigerated truck, and a Honduran immigrant fleeing gang violence applies for asylum in the US.
Dairy Farmer: Guy Martin travels to a remote island in the Hebrides to live and work on a dairy farm during calving season - after learning the ropes, he milks a herd of cows by himself.
The sometimes outrageous, always jocular motoring show with Clarkson and chums. Jeremy celebrates the 50th birthday of the Jaguar E-type with a typically low-key birthday party.
Ciao! Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May travel to Italy for a series of challenges and increasingly loud arguments. Geordie randomist Ross Noble guests.
Beer and Cider: The lovable bikers explore how our love affair with beer and cider has evolved over time. They also visit a cider festival, where they press their own.
Sicily and Puglia: A colourful culinary tour of the Mediterranean. Rick continues his travels in Sicily and enjoys the ambience of Catania's busy fish market - he then travels on to Puglia.
Celebrity guests Bez, Motsi Mabuse, Shazia Mirza and Steve Pemberton reveal extraordinary stories about themselves, but are they telling the truth or making it all up?
TV presenters Alex Jones and Karim Zeroual, comedian Jessica Knappett, and Judge Rob Rinder think they've got what it takes to take home one of the Pointless star's prizes this time.
Retired athletes Steve Cram and Jeanette Kwakye hope that they won't be for the high jump when they join comedians Lloyd Griffith and Josie Long on the lovable quiz show.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: Jason Manford is host as Jess Phillips MP and comedian Glenn Moore join team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop to delve into the news.
Upside Down: Sandi Toksvig is joined by Aisling Bea, Urzila Carlson, Romesh Ranganathan and Alan Davies for an upside-down episode.
Don't Like Them to Go Bang: The five comics go undercover as they try to impress Greg Davies with their attempts at high-level espionage.
Dr Dara puts the week's news under the microscope as Josh Widdicombe, Gary Delaney, Tiffany Stevenson and Romesh Ranganathan join the regulars to dissect the big headlines.
Bruno Tonioli, Adam Buxton, Kirsty Wark and Rob Beckett join brazen bluffers David Mitchell and Lee Mack to tell tall tales and uncomfortable truths. Rob Brydon presents.
Celebrity guests Bez, Motsi Mabuse, Shazia Mirza and Steve Pemberton reveal extraordinary stories about themselves, but are they telling the truth or making it all up?
Josh Widdicombe and James Acaster host more celebs tasked with making the absurd plausible - their guests this time are Frank Skinner, Rosie Jones, Jon Richardson and Esther Manito.
Josh Widdicombe and James Acaster welcome more celebrities to the realm of the (im)possible - their guests this time are Liza Tarbuck, Judi Love, Josh Pugh and Amy Gledhill.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Thousands of dollars of cartel cash are found hidden in an engine, an intoxicated 17-year-old requires urgent medical treatment, and cocaine is found in bags of rubbish.
Friday Nights Are A Bad Cocktail: The drug squad makes a pre-dawn arrest on a worker travelling over the border from Spain on his moped, and trainee officers undergo pepper spray training.
Brace, Brace, Brace: Customs officers spring into action after news of a sinking boat - on land, Sergeant Steve uses good old-fashioned legwork to track down two stolen scooters.
Documentary series. A new V/Line VLocity train is driven to its limits to pass its final test, and the ageing Savannahlander railcar faces one of Australia's roughest tracks.
Puglia and Corfu: In Corfu, Rick attends a family feast, visits the island's kitchens and learns how to make authentic Greek food.
A shipment of meth disguised as concrete is intercepted, and officers interview a Haitian immigrant who has crossed nine countries on foot to reach America.
Millions of dollars' worth of marijuana is found hidden in a shipment of mattresses, and officers detain a mother and daughter who have been smoking meth.
Truck Rescue: Guy joins a multi-generational family business who rescue crashed or broken-down HGVs as he learns the physics of truck recovery and operates a crane during a complex rescue.
Motoring madness. Jeremy Clarkson and Richard Hammond find second-hand bargains at the price of the Nissan Pixo and F1 star Sebastian Vettel tries out the Reasonably Priced Car.
Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May try to make train travel cheaper, faster and more interesting - good luck with that, lads. The very funny Rowan Atkinson guests.
Pies: The hairy duo celebrate British food. They find out who ate all the pies on the football pitch and discover the story behind some of the nation's best loved pies.
Puglia and Corfu: In Corfu, Rick attends a family feast, visits the island's kitchens and learns how to make authentic Greek food.
Rob Brydon and team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell are joined by guests Chizzy Akudolu, Simon Gregson, Michelle Visage and Henning Wehn.
TV presenters Alex Jones and Karim Zeroual, comedian Jessica Knappett, and Judge Rob Rinder think they've got what it takes to take home one of the Pointless star's prizes this time.
Retired athletes Steve Cram and Jeanette Kwakye hope that they won't be for the high jump when they join comedians Lloyd Griffith and Josie Long on the lovable quiz show.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: Phil Wang is host as guest panellists Baroness Ruth Davidson and writer and broadcaster Richard Osman join team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop to delve into the news.
United: Sandi Toksvig's guests - Nabil Abdulrashid, Bill Bailey, Victoria Coren Mitchell and Alan Davies - are all in it together for this united episode.
Think About the Spirit: It's the grand final - peas, teas, and absent keys feature as Ed Gamble, Jo Brand, Katy Wix and Rose Matafeo vie to go home with Greg Davies's massive face.
